findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement
findings.description: The North Star metric for SmartNotify is the Daily Active Users (DAU), which reflects the number of unique users engaging with the notification system daily. This metric is crucial as it directly correlates with user retention and satisfaction.
assumptions: User engagement is a key driver of product success.
recommendations: Monitor DAU trends weekly to assess user engagement.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that result in user engagement. It is a primary KPI that indicates the effectiveness of notification content and timing.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better notification relevance.
recommendations: A/B test notification formats to improve CTR.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: The Unsubscribe Rate indicates the percentage of users opting out of notifications. A lower rate suggests that users find the notifications valuable.
assumptions: Lower unsubscribe rates correlate with higher user satisfaction.
recommendations: Investigate reasons for unsubscribes to enhance user retention.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Average Notifications per User
findings.description: The Average Notifications per User metric tracks the number of notifications sent to each user. This metric should not increase significantly to avoid notification fatigue.
assumptions: Maintaining a low average prevents user fatigue.
recommendations: Limit notifications to a maximum of 25 per user.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: 95th Percentile Delivery Latency
findings.description: The 95th Percentile Delivery Latency measures the time taken to deliver notifications. This metric must not exceed 200ms to ensure timely user engagement.
recommendations: Implement asynchronous writes to reduce latency.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Preference Data
findings.description: There is a gap in collecting data on user preferences for notification timing and content. This data is essential for optimizing notification delivery.
assumptions: Understanding user preferences will enhance engagement.

evidence.excerpt: ML model should learn per-user preferences for delivery timing.

recommendations: Implement user surveys or feedback mechanisms to gather preference data.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Notification Format A/B Testing
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different notification formats to determine which format yields the highest CTR and lowest unsubscribe rate.
assumptions: Different formats will have varying impacts on user engagement.
recommendations: Run A/B tests with a minimum sample size of 1000 users per variant.

summary: The analysis identifies the North Star metric as Daily Active Users, with supporting KPIs including Notification CTR and Unsubscribe Rate. Guardrail metrics ensure user experience is maintained. Gaps in user preference data collection and opportunities for A/B testing are also highlighted for further exploration.
